title: Peak flow diary review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.
red_flags.rationale: Supports asthma-control monitoring and review for peak-flow diary review; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.6.4 — monitoring asthma control and uncontrolled asthma

examination.text: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.
examination.rationale: Supports peak expiratory flow in objective asthma assessment for peak-flow diary review; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Diary dates, technique, best and predicted value, readings, variability, symptoms, reliever use, triggers, adherence, interpretation, treatment and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for peak-flow diary review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
